summary: Emotional Oakland set with soaring Iko‑Mona and tight China>Rider
blurb: The show rides a heartfelt tribute mood, delivering a blistering Althea, a standout Touch of Grey, and an emotionally charged second set highlighted by the Iko → Mona jam and soaring China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider. Jerry’s crystal‑clear tone sits front‑and‑center, while the rhythm section stays tight and magical.
review: On October 27, 1991, the Grateful Dead turned the Oakland Coliseum into a moving tribute to Bill Graham. From the opening burst of "Sugar Magnolia" through a powerful first set featuring "Althea" and "Touch of Grey," the band kept an emotional intensity that only grew in the second half. The centerpiece was the transcendent "Iko → Mona" jam, where Jerry’s guitar soars with crystal‑clear tone and Brent’s Korg M1 textures weave into the groove. That momentum carried straight into a soaring "China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider," followed by a beautifully executed suite of "Ship Of Fools," drums, space, and an uplifting finish with "The Wheel" through "Good Lovin'". Guest guitarist Carlos Santana added tasteful leads, further lifting the night’s energy. Across all recordings the performance is praised as one of the strongest of the ’91 tour, with a rhythm section described as “tight and magical” and Mickey Hart’s kinetic drumming adding extra fire. For fans seeking the definitive listening experience, the Martinson soundboard capture (gd91-10-27.sbd.martinson.537.sbeok.shnf) offers crystal‑clear mix, excellent instrument separation, and all the emotional highs of this unforgettable night.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings consistently use positive language ('emotionally charged', 'blistering', 'masterful', 'soaring'), highlight the same standout moments (Iko → Mona, China > Rider, Althea) and describe the set as a heartfelt tribute. No negative sentiment appears across analyses, indicating strong collective approval.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard with crisp mix, clear instrument separation, and captures the emotional peaks of the show better than any other source.

key_highlights: Stunning "Iko → Mona" jam that lifts the entire second set.
"China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider" executed with soaring interplay.
Jerry’s crystal‑clear guitar tone, especially on the Iko → Mona solo.
Guest lead work by Carlos Santana adds a special flavor to the night.

band_performance.Jerry: Crystal‑clear tone, louder in the mix and especially powerful during the Iko → Mona solo.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboards provide equal‑part texture to the guitars; Brent’s Korg M1 sounds shine on Mona and Santana’s guest leads are praised as tasteful.
band_performance.Mickey: Kinetic energy, often described as “kicking over his kit,” driving the rhythm section with magical tightness.
